title: I See Myself. That's the Only One That Counts.
subtitle: An affirmation for mid-life visibility on your own terms
excerpt: A short affirmation for the mid-life shift away from external visibility toward self-recognition. For the moment when culture is watching elsewhere and you need a counter-statement.

content.text: A short affirmation for the mid-life shift in whose gaze matters. When external visibility reduces, the question is whether your own recognition is sufficient. This affirmation says it is.
Say it in the mirror, in the morning, three times. Look at yourself while you say it.
I see myself. That's the only one that counts.

The affirmation is a redirect. Not a denial that external visibility matters in some contexts, but a deliberate move of the centre of gravity. The person who sees you most clearly is the one in the mirror. That recognition is not a consolation prize. It is the more reliable and the more durable one.
